pr.title: Improve matching in Melee source files
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Large batch matching-improvement PR across 57 src/melee files, spanning effects, fighters, game mode/result/menu/interface/stage/library/toy code. The PR raised GALE01 matched code by 9176 bytes to 70.39% (+0.24%) and matched data by 712 bytes to 41.33% (+0.06%), with 13 new matches, 123 improved unmatched items, and no reported broken matches or regressions in the saved-baseline report. It also emphasized a stricter local regression gate for changes/changes_all so matching metrics fail on backward movement. Review focused on avoiding fake matches and matching-only shortcuts, preferring real source structure such as loops, avoiding pragmas and M2C_FIELD where possible, and reverting unrelated tooling changes.
what_changed: Improved matching across a broad batch of src/melee source files: 57 changed files, 7984 additions, 7198 deletions.
Reported GALE01 progress from baseline dae3629 to PR commit ff551b3: matched code 70.39% (+9176 bytes) and matched data 41.33% (+712 bytes).

Produced 13 new full matches, including if_2FC93 fn_802FDA78, ftafterimage .data, grkinokoroute .data, grkongo .rodata/.sdata, ftchangeparam ftCo_800CF6E8, ifstock .sdata2, mpGetSpeed, gmstaffroll fn_801AA854, and grCorneria_801DD534.

Improved 123 unmatched items with no reported regressions; large examples include mplib .data (+3040 bytes), gmresult fn_80175DC8 (+2751 bytes), gm_18A5 fn_8018B090 (+1450 bytes), grkongo grKongo_801D7134 (+1408 bytes), and gmresult fn_80174B4C (+940 bytes).

Reworked gmresult fn_80175DC8 from a best-effort/nonmatching implementation into a much more detailed JObj/results-panel setup function, including a static u8 lookup table lbl_803B7B18 and explicit HSD_JObj lookup/assignment patterns.
Changed effect spawning code in efasync.c and efsync.c to introduce temporary f32 variables before HSD_JObjSetRotationY calls, a local matching tactic visible in repeated M_PI_2/rotation cases.

Changed ftCo_8009F834 to use Vec3 stack temporaries instead of separate scalar x/y/z locals, simplified some efAsync_Spawn argument lists, and replaced a suspicious ft_data pointer expression with indexed int access.

Expanded ftCo_800A2718 in ftCo_0A01.c from inline helper checks into explicit item iteration and stage-specific switch logic for STORY, ZEBES, and ONETT, with dont_inline pragmas present in the excerpt.
Changed matching shape in stage and menu/UI files, especially grkongo, grcorneria, grvenom, grgreens, ifstock, mndiagram, mndiagram3, mnvibration, and toy-related files.
PR body described a strict local saved-baseline regression gate so changes/changes_all fail if any unit, section, or function metric moves backward.
smart_moves: Used saved-baseline comparison as the central validation artifact, explicitly listing new matches, improved unmatched items, broken matches, and regressions.
Kept the pushed PR report no-regression: 0 broken matches and 0 regressions in unmatched items, despite touching many systems.
Targeted high-impact files where small source-shape changes yielded large matching gains, especially mplib .data, gmresult functions, and several stage functions.
Converted scalar x/y/z stack locals into Vec3 stack temporaries in ftCo_8009F834, which is a reusable tactic when matching code appears to copy or mutate vector structs as whole objects.
Introduced explicit stack temporaries for HSD_JObj pointer lookup and assignment in gmresult, matching the call pattern around lb_80011E24 and stored result pointers.
Removed some noisy best-effort comments in gmresult and replaced them with concrete source structure, static data, and explicit JObj setup code.
Recognized that matching data sections can be as important as code, with .data/.rodata/.sdata/.sdata2 improvements called out in the report.
Validated locally with python3 configure.py --require-protos and ninja changes_all.
decomp_lessons: For matching PRs, include a saved-baseline report with before/after metrics and explicit no-regression sections; reviewers can judge large broad changes more safely.
Avoid relying on pragmas as a first resort; reviewers asked to avoid #pragma usage where possible, including global_optimizer off and dont_inline patterns.
Repeated hand-unrolled code should be rechecked as likely loops; several reviewer comments flagged repeated player/result/stage code as probably for loops.
M2C_FIELD and M2C_BITWISE-style artifacts should be removed when real struct fields, types, or control flow can be recovered.
Do not accept matches that look like fake source regressions: a reviewer specifically asked to undo a lb_00F9.c change because the original HSD_JObj form was likely how the source was written.
Asserts mentioning jobj.h may indicate code inlined or included from jobj.c; future work should compare against HSD/JObj implementation patterns before local rewrites.
Data table reconstruction can unlock substantial matching gains in functions that initialize UI/model state.
Wide matching sweeps should avoid unrelated tooling edits unless the PR explicitly owns them; changes_fmt.py changes were requested to be reverted.
naming_conventions: Effect dispatch/spawn functions used descriptive names already present in the source: efAsync_Dispatch and efSync_Spawn.
Temporary stack names in partially decompiled code retained address-like names such as sp108, sp104, spFC, spA8, and sp84 when better semantics were not evident.
Unknown or address-derived functions retained project-style names such as fn_80175DC8, fn_80161154, grKongo_801D7134, grCorneria_801DED50, and ifStock_802F7EFC.
Static data labels retained address-style names such as lbl_803B7B18 and lbl_8046DBE8 when evidence for semantic names was absent.
Struct field names in unknown structs retained offset-style names such as x24, x28, x34, x94, player_data, x0_0, and jobjs.
Stage enum names visible in the diff used all-caps identifiers such as STORY, ZEBES, and ONETT.
Fighter and stage file names followed subsystem prefixes: ftCo_, ftKb_, ftYs_, grKongo_, grCorneria_, grVenom_, ifStock_, mnDiagram_, and gm_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use extra f32 temporaries before floating-point API calls when register allocation requires a copied value, as in efAsync_Dispatch/efSync_Spawn assigning rot_y or half_pi to f32_1/va_f32_1 before HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
Try whole-struct Vec3 stack assignments instead of scalar field locals when stack layout or copy code suggests vector temporaries.
Use PAD_STACK to tune stack size when source structure is otherwise understood, but keep it as a matching aid rather than semantic proof.
Recover static const data tables when large functions reference compact lookup data; gmresult added static const u8 lbl_803B7B18[40][2].
When repeated switch/case or copy-pasted blocks appear, test equivalent for-loop forms because reviewers repeatedly suspected loops in gm_1601.c, gmresult.c, and grkongo.c.
For HSD_JObj lookup helpers such as lb_80011E24, test whether the original source used local JObj temporaries initialized to jobj and then assigned into global/struct fields.
Avoid fake matches that distort likely original API usage; reviewer evidence says preserving an HSD_JObj form may be preferable even if another form matches locally.
Prefer replacing M2C_FIELD/M2C_BITWISE artifacts with real fields, casts, arrays, or typed temporaries once enough structure is known.
Pragmas like global_optimizer off and dont_inline may produce a match but are discouraged unless no source-shaped alternative exists.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.lesson: Do not use #pragma global_optimizer off as a default matching solution; attempt source-shaped control flow first.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: This code is most likely a for loop, same with the repeated code below here.
review_feedback.lesson: Repeated player_standings/team_standings flag setup should be tested as a loop rather than manual pointer increments or unrolled code.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: This is probably a for loop? idk about the out-of-order 6/7 but good to check anyways.
review_feedback.lesson: Large repeated JObj initialization blocks may hide loops or table-driven indexing, even when some indices appear out of order.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Try to remove any M2C_FIELD if possibe.
review_feedback.lesson: M2C artifacts are acceptable only as temporary scaffolding; reviewers expect real struct typing where possible.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.lesson: Stage logic with repeated near-identical operations should be loop-tested.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Any "jobj.h" asserts are indications of an included/run jobj.c function.
review_feedback.lesson: Use assert strings and source file references as clues for inlined/included library routines.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.feedback: Undo this, this is likely a regression/"fake match", the HSD_Jobj is the way it was written.
review_feedback.lesson: A byte match is not enough if it makes the code less faithful to likely original source.

review_feedback.author: jurrejelle
review_feedback.lesson: Keep matching PRs scoped; avoid incidental tooling changes unless needed and reviewed separately.

searchable_terms: Improve matching in Melee source files
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that reverted or refined the #pragma global_optimizer off or dont_inline usage introduced around gm_1601.c and ftCo_0A01.c.
Search for subsequent cleanup of M2C_FIELD/M2C_BITWISE artifacts in gmresult.c and related ResultsPlayerData structures.
Check whether gmresult fn_80175DC8 was later converted from repeated switch/JObj blocks into loops or table-driven initialization.
Search grvenom and HSD/JObj assert-string discussions for guidance on recognizing included jobj.c functions.
Find follow-up changes to lb_00F9.c to see how the flagged HSD_JObj fake-match concern was resolved.
Look for later changes to tools/changes_fmt.py or the regression gate mentioned in this PR.
